---
title: "Label read-mode (two-tone outline) — report"
author: "Yun Jiang"
date: "2026-07-22"
format:
  html:
    html-math-method: mathjax
include-in-header:
  text: |
    <link rel="icon" href="data:image/svg+xml,<svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 100 100'><text y='.9em' font-size='90'>🏷️</text></svg>">
---

# Status

Done and shipped. CI green (Build ✓, Test ✓). Plan:
[`20260722-143930_label-read-mode.plan.qmd`](20260722-143930_label-read-mode.plan.qmd).

# What was done

Replaced the backtick **global-opacity dim** (which coupled label contrast to the
background and vanished on dark surfaces) with a **render-style switch**:

- **Read-mode (backtick)** drops the pill and draws each label as a **two-tone
  outline** — yellow fill + black stroke — at **full opacity**. Stroke gives the
  edge on light backgrounds; fill pops on dark ones → crisp on any background,
  while the missing pill lets the text behind show through.
- **Base pill mode unchanged** (already legible on dark backgrounds).

Files:

- `src/HuntAndPeck/Views/HintCanvas.cs` — caches a `BuildGeometry` outline per
  label (once, like the existing `FormattedText` cache), a frozen `_outlinePen`
  (thickness `max(1, fontSize/9)`), a new `ReadMode` DP whose change callback
  re-renders every visual, and a `RenderHint` branch (base = `DrawRoundedRectangle`
  pill + `DrawText`; read = `DrawGeometry(fill, pen, geom)`).
- `src/HuntAndPeck/ViewModels/OverlayViewModel.cs` — `ReadMode`/`ToggleReadMode`;
  `LabelOpacity` now reflects **only** suspend (suspend still dims, by design).
- `src/HuntAndPeck/Services/OverlayKeyboardHook.cs` — enum `ToggleOpacity` →
  `ToggleReadMode`; `VK_OEM_3` → `ToggleReadMode`; dispatch → `ToggleReadMode()`.
- `src/HuntAndPeck/Views/OverlayView.xaml` — `ReadMode="{Binding ReadMode}"`.
- `src/HuntAndPeck.Tests/Services/OverlayKeyboardHookTest.cs` —
  `Backtick_TogglesReadMode`.
- `src/HuntAndPeck/Services/OverlayActionConfig.cs` — legend mentions
  `` ` `` = read-through, `\` = suspend.
- `CLAUDE.md`, `CHANGELOG.md` — behavior docs updated.

# Key correctness note

A literal single-color **hollow** outline cannot be crisp on both light and dark
backgrounds (black stroke vanishes on dark, white on light). Read-mode is therefore
**two-tone** — the evidence basis ([NN/g, 2015](https://www.nngroup.com/articles/text-over-images/))
and the WPF API used
([Microsoft, "Create Outlined Text"](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/desktop/wpf/advanced/how-to-create-outlined-text)).

# Verification

- **CI** (`.github/workflows/build.yml`, run 29897928460): Build ✓, Test ✓ (xUnit,
  incl. `Backtick_TogglesReadMode`), artifact uploaded.
- **No stale refs**: `grep` for `LabelsOpaque`/`ToggleOpacity`/`_labelsOpaque` in
  `src/` returns none; `LabelOpacity` remains only for the suspend dim.
- **Perf**: outline geometry cached once per label (not per render); no `Effect`.
  No regression to the 1000+ label budget.
- **Runtime (pending, Windows box)**: drop at
  `…/hap-read-mode/hap.exe` — backtick on a dark background should keep labels
  crisp (yellow fill + black stroke, no pill) with the text behind readable;
  backtick again restores pills. Base mode unchanged. Suspend still dims.

# Commit

`a26325d` on `master` (jiangyun-fun/hunt-and-peck).
